prompt: A motor mounting bracket for a NEMA 17 stepper with 4x M3 clearance holes, 3mm gussets, and a 22mm center bore. Plate 50x50mm, 6mm thick.
A robot end-effector gripper jaw: 60mm long, 20mm wide, 12mm thick, with two M4 mounting holes at one end and a 5mm cable channel slot along the top face.
A 2020 aluminum extrusion corner bracket: 40x40mm legs, 20mm wide, 4mm thick, with one M5 clearance hole per leg and lightening holes.
A differential drive robot chassis base plate, 180x120mm, 4mm thick, with two 30x20mm rectangular motor cutouts on the long sides and four M2.5 clearance holes on a 58x49mm grid for a Raspberry Pi.
A 6-DOF robot arm link: hollow rectangular tube 120mm long, 40x30mm cross-section with 4mm walls, and a 22mm bearing seat bore at each end.
A camera gimbal mount plate 60x40mm, 5mm thick, with four M2 screw holes inset 5mm from the corners and two 20mm long, 4mm wide vibration isolation slots.
A pulley with a 20mm bore, 60mm OD, 15mm width, and a 6mm wide, 3.5mm deep keyway slot in the bore.
A 3D-printable planetary gear carrier disc, 70mm diameter, 8mm thick, with three 10mm planet gear bores on a 44mm PCD and a 12mm sun gear center bore.
A heat sink base for a motor driver, 50x50mm footprint, 5mm thick base, with 10 fins 20mm tall and 2mm thick, and two mounting ears with M3 holes.
A cable chain link for a 15x10mm cable bundle: outer body 24x18mm, 20mm long, 2.5mm walls, open on top, with an 8mm hinge pin hole through both side walls near one end.
